title: Blood product discussion documentation
source_review_basis: full authoritative documents inspected and archived; item-level retain/rewrite/remove/add comparisons recorded

assessment.text: Supports clinician-documented discussion of blood products, risks, benefits, alternatives, and patient preferences without recommending transfusion.
assessment.rationale: The exact ASA section supports documenting a clinician-led blood-product risk, benefit, alternative, and preference discussion without recommending transfusion.

source.title: Practice Guidelines for Perioperative Blood Management
source.population: Patients undergoing surgical and other invasive procedures where blood loss, anemia, coagulopathy, or transfusion may be relevant.
source.setting: Preoperative, intraoperative, and postoperative blood-management assessment.
source.exclusions: Exact for record review, prior transfusion and reactions, bleeding or thrombotic history, laboratory review, patient preferences, and clinician discussion of blood-product risks and benefits. Transfusion thresholds and management are not mapped.
source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports clinician-documented discussion of blood products, risks, benefits, alternatives, and patient preferences without recommending transfusion.

assessment.text: Supports identity, disposition, medical, surgical and anesthetic history, medicines, allergies and reactions, fasting, advance directives, airway and cardiopulmonary examination, tests, consultations, ASA status, plan including postanesthesia and pain care, and consent.
assessment.rationale: The exact ASA preanesthesia documentation section supports risk, airway, cardiorespiratory, reviewed-investigation, consultation, plan, and consent fields for Blood product discussion documentation; each remains clinician-entered and conditional on assessment.

source.title: Statement on Documentation of Anesthesia Care
source.population: Patients receiving anesthesia services across preanesthesia, intraoperative, and postanesthesia phases.
source.setting: Anesthesia care continuum and anesthesia medical records.
source.exclusions: Exact for documentation fields and continuity of anesthesia records. It supports recording clinician-assessed facts and completed plans, not generating management, medicines, doses, procedures, referrals, discharge, or urgency.
source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports identity, disposition, medical, surgical and anesthetic history, medicines, allergies and reactions, fasting, advance directives, airway and cardiopulmonary examination, tests, consultations, ASA status, plan including postanesthesia and pain care, and consent.

sources_used.title: Practice Guidelines for Perioperative Blood Management
Statement on Documentation of Anesthesia Care

additions.final_wording: Supports clinician-documented discussion of blood products, risks, benefits, alternatives, and patient preferences without recommending transfusion.
